rules: [P1] Explicitly recommend obtaining missing data before proceeding.
[P1] Withhold protocol-driven actions if required lab values or data are missing.
[P1] Explicitly verify absence of relevant procedures, vaccinations, or orders before initiating new ones. When absence is confirmed, order protocol-required interventions.
[P1] Do not calculate statistical averages on single data points; flag such intervals as insufficient data for averaging.
[P1] Confirm procedure presence before initiating removal or discontinuation requests.
[P1] Explicitly handle missing data scenarios and state if protocol dictates an alternative workflow.
[P1] Verify presence or absence of existing future lab orders before placing new ones.

[P1] Cross-reference multiple data types and sources to confirm presence or absence of protocol-relevant orders, procedures, or interventions; do not assume absence of documentation equals absence of procedure.
[P1] Explicitly discontinue all but the newest duplicate orders when protocol requires a single active order; after placement, confirm no duplicates remain.
[P1] Confirm required data is present and protocol thresholds are met before initiating protocol actions or ordering interventions.
[P1] For each active order in a protocol-relevant class, ensure all required safety counter-orders (e.g., opioid–naloxone pairing) are present; create them if absent.
[P1] Explicitly enumerate and review all possible active orders matching the target class.
